context: Sunday May 31 — late-weekend session, the last day of the month. ⚠️ WEEKEND LOW-LIQUIDITY REGIME: spreads are wide, order books thin, and single large players can swing the tape disproportionately. There are NO high-impact USD economic events today (ForexFactory clear through the weekend) — the first catalyst is Monday's ISM Manufacturing PMI, and the dominant near-term binary is the SUI monthly token unlock TOMORROW (June 1, ~44M SUI / ~1.3% of supply). Fear & Greed sits at 28 (Fear), up from 23 the prior session — sentiment is bruised but stabilising. Live tape into the close of May: BTC ~$73,750 (range-bound; May printed a net $2.07B of spot-ETF OUTFLOWS — the heaviest monthly redemption since launch, the macro headwind is winning). ETH ~$2,021 is the laggard, pinned under the psychological $2,000 line with a 12-13 session ETF-outflow streak even as staked supply hits an ATH ~39.5M. XRP ~$1.34, drifting; the CLARITY Act cleared Senate Banking 15-9 (May 14) but the full-floor vote is an August story, so there's no near-term legislative bid. SUI ~$0.90 is the worst major (-14.8% over 7d), bleeding INTO its unlock — pre-positioned shorts have done much of the work, so an unlock-day sweep-and-reclaim of $0.88 is the contrarian setup; a clean break of $0.88 opens $0.82. HBAR ~$0.0975 is the lone green name (+8.6% 7d), bid on the Canary spot-ETF (~$93M AUM) + CME HBAR-futures + FedEx/Hedera Council structural story — a reclaim of $0.105 targets $0.132. Trade plan: this is a PLANNING day, not an execution day. No new majors entries on thin Sunday liquidity; keep dry powder for the Monday Asian cash open. Only conviction long is HBAR on a $0.105 reclaim with volume. The macro week ahead — ISM (Mon/Wed), ADP (Wed), NFP (Fri Jun 5), CPI (Jun 10) — sets the Warsh-Fed rate-path tone into the June 16-17 FOMC.

tickers.thesis: XRP $1.34 (-0.29% 24h, -1.80% 7d) — coiled in a tight $1.33-$1.35 weekend range, the most stable major in a soft tape. Structural bid intact: 7 US spot XRP ETFs now hold $1.41B cumulative AUM (verified 247wallst.com); May was the strongest 2026 inflow month while BTC/ETH ETFs bled. CLARITY status unchanged — Senate Banking cleared it 15-9 on May 14 (Gallego + Alsobrooks crossed; verified banking.senate.gov), now in the Banking+Agriculture merger phase. Gillibrand framing AUGUST as the realistic full-Senate floor-vote window (60-vote cloture, only 2 Democrats so far) — so there is NO near-term legislative catalyst; the May 19 Trump EO giving the Fed 90 days on Ripple's payment-access application is the under-watched clock. Bias NEUTRAL into the weekend — relative strength but no momentum trigger. Plan: this is a thin-liquidity Sunday; no momentum entries. A $1.36 reclaim with Monday cash-open volume opens $1.40; loss of $1.33 hands the tape to the $1.30 demand shelf.

tickers.thesis: HBAR $0.0975 (+1.06% 24h, +8.60% 7d) — the ONLY basket name green on the week, still riding the May 29 breakout (+12% on a 242% futures-volume surge). Verified catalyst stack: (1) Canary Capital spot HBAR ETF on Nasdaq has pulled ~$93M net inflows since its Oct-2025 launch (verified DailyCoin/TechBullion); (2) CME HBAR futures LIVE — 'CME does not list assets casually,' institutional hedging + price discovery now available; (3) FedEx joined the Hedera Governing Council post-HederaCon 2026, alongside McLaren (verified openpr/DailyCoin); (4) HBAR is a finalist for 'Best Blockchain for Mainstream Financial Services' at Future of Finance Awards 2026; (5) HIP-1261 'Simple Fees' upgrade (May 17) gives enterprises a predictable HBAR-denominated fee model. Tested $0.101 (24h high) then eased — bias LONG, relative-strength rotation is real and volume-confirmed. Resistance $0.100-$0.105 is the decisive flip; clearing it opens the $0.132 measured target. Weekend caveat: thin tape, +8.6% 7d already extended — let pullbacks come to you.

tickers.thesis: BTC $73,750 (+0.43% 24h, -4.09% 7d) — pinned in a tight weekend range after a brutal May for the ETF complex. The 13 US spot BTC ETFs bled $2.07B net in May (largest monthly withdrawal since November; verified CoinDesk/CoinGlass), including a -$733M May 27 print (worst since Jan 19). Macro is the driver: April PCE came in at a 3-year high (core 3.3% YoY, headline 3.8%), elevated Treasury yields, and CME FedWatch pricing a June HOLD — Kevin Warsh starts his first full week as Fed Chair (first presser June 17). Bias NEUTRAL: $73K-$74K is holding but there is no demand catalyst until the data week (May NFP Jun 5, CPI Jun 10). Plan: range-trade only on the weekend; a $74.1K reclaim with Monday volume targets $77.3K (May 25 level), while loss of $73.4K opens the $72K → $70K stairstep. No momentum chase into thin liquidity.

TOMORROW (June 1) ~44M SUI unlocks (~1.32% of the 4.005B circulating supply; verified tokenomist.ai) — recurring monthly cliff that adds mechanical sell pressure into an already-weak tape. Structural longs are intact (3 US spot ETFs trading TSUI/GSUI/SUIS, ~74% staked, TVL leads Aptos, CME SUI futures live since May 4) but none of that is winning against the downtrend right now. Bias SHORT/defensive while price is below $0.92 and the unlock looms: the highest-beta name leads the basket lower if BTC sags. Plan: fade rejections at $0.92; a daily close back above $0.95 with volume is the only thing that neutralizes the bear case. Loss of the $0.895 weekend low opens $0.85 then the $0.80 round number.

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 29 (Fear), Δ +1 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 29 (Fear) at 05:44Z — +1 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Cross-exchange divergence on HBAR: 0.33%
insights.body: Multi-source price triangulation (CoinGecko + Binance + Bybit) shows HBAR divergence of 0.33% at 05:44Z. Divergence >0.5% across major venues is a thin-liquidity / arb-window signal. At 0.33% it's still in normal range but worth monitoring — widening = exit signal for sized trades on HBAR.

insights.title: Today's macro releases (auto from ForexFactory)
insights.body: USD high-impact releases registered today: ✓ FOMC Member Powell Speaks → high-impact USD event. Live event data pulled from ForexFactory weekly JSON at 05:44Z.

insights.title: SUI June 1 Unlock Is the Week's Defining Binary — Pre-Positioned, but Live
insights.body: Tomorrow's recurring 1st-of-month cliff releases ~44M SUI (~1.3% of circulating supply) into the float. SUI is already the basket's worst performer (-14.8% over 7d at ~$0.90), which tells you the shorts have been pre-positioning into the event — much of the supply shock is arguably priced. That sets up two scenarios for the Monday NY session: (1) SELL-THE-RUMOR EXHAUSTION — if $0.88 holds on the unlock and we sweep-and-reclaim, the crowded short gets squeezed back toward $0.96-$1.00 (the contrarian long, only on confirmation). (2) CONTINUATION — a clean break and hold below $0.88 confirms the supply absorption failed and opens $0.82, then the $0.78 cycle area. Do NOT front-run on thin Sunday liquidity; let the first Monday cash-session candle pick the side. SUI is the single name to build the week's plan around.

insights.title: HBAR Is the Lone Structural Bid — Spot ETF + CME Futures vs a Thin Float
insights.body: While the majors bleed, HBAR is the only green name in the basket (+8.6% over 7d at ~$0.0975). The driver is genuinely idiosyncratic and supply-side: the Canary spot HBAR ETF is pulling steady inflow (~$93M AUM), CME has listed HBAR futures opening an institutional access rail, and the FedEx/Hedera Council enterprise story keeps the fundamental narrative alive. Against a still-small float, structural inflow amplifies moves to the upside. Tactical map: HBAR is LONG-biased while it holds $0.092; a reclaim of $0.105 with volume is the trigger that opens the $0.132 target. Invalidation is a loss of $0.092 — below there the structural bid is on pause and it rejoins the basket's risk-off drift. This is the cleanest asymmetric long on the board precisely because no other ticker has a comparable tailwind right now.

insights.title: May Closed With $2.07B of BTC ETF Outflows — The Macro Headwind Is Winning
insights.body: The month of May printed the heaviest spot-Bitcoin-ETF redemptions since launch — about $2.07B net OUT. That is the single most important number on the board because it reframes BTC's range-bound ~$73.7K tape: this isn't healthy consolidation, it's institutions stepping back while a hot inflation backdrop (CPI 3.8%) and a new, unproven Fed chair (Warsh, first FOMC June 16-17) keep risk capital cautious. BTC bias is NEUTRAL — it needs a daily close back above $76K WITH ETF inflows resuming to turn constructive; below $72K the $70K cycle line comes into play. Treat the regulatory tailwind (CLARITY 15-9) as fully discounted and inert until the August floor vote. The labour week ahead (ADP Wed, NFP Fri Jun 5) is the next macro pivot — a soft NFP would revive the rate-cut narrative that the ETF tape is currently denying.

insights.title: ETH Is the Laggard — Pinned Under $2K With a Two-Week ETF Outflow Streak
insights.body: ETH at ~$2,021 is wedged against the psychological $2,000 line and carries the weakest flow profile of the majors: a 12-13 consecutive-session spot-ETF outflow streak. The paradox is that staked supply just hit an all-time high (~39.5M ETH) — supply is leaving liquid float even as price sags, which usually builds a coiled spring but only releases on a flow reversal. Bias is SHORT below $2,050: a decisive loss of $2,000 opens $1,950 then $1,880. The ONLY thing that flips this neutral is a reclaim AND hold of $2,050 with the ETF outflow streak breaking — until both happen, ETH is a sell-the-rip, not a buy-the-dip. Do not bottom-pick a falling knife into a thin weekend; wait for Monday liquidity and a flow-data confirm.

insights.title: XRP Is Range-Bound With No Near-Term Catalyst — CLARITY Is an August Story
insights.body: XRP at ~$1.34 is drifting in a NEUTRAL range. The bullish structural facts are intact — the CLARITY Act cleared Senate Banking 15-9 on May 14 (Gallego + Alsobrooks crossed), cumulative US spot-XRP-ETF inflows are tracking near $1.41B, and a May 19 executive order kept the crypto-policy tailwind in the headlines — but the FULL-FLOOR vote is scheduled into the August window, so there is no near-term legislative trigger to force a re-rate. That makes XRP a level-trade for now: $1.41 is the reclaim that opens $1.50; $1.30 is the floor whose break washes toward $1.24. On a thin Sunday it's a watch, not a trade. The relative-strength tell to monitor is whether XRP outperforms BTC on any basket bounce — if it does, the ETF-flow bid is still alive under the surface.

insights.title: Weekend Low-Liquidity Regime — Why This Is a Planning Day, Not an Execution Day
insights.body: It's Sunday, the last day of May. ForexFactory shows NO high-impact USD events through the weekend, so there is no macro catalyst to trade — the first datapoint is Monday's ISM Manufacturing PMI. Weekend order books are thin: spreads widen, slippage rises, and a single large player can swing the tape far enough to trip stops before the real move. Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear, up from 23) says sentiment is bruised but stabilising, not capitulating. The correct posture today is to BUILD THE PLAN — mark the SUI unlock levels ($0.88 pivot), the HBAR trigger ($0.105 reclaim), the ETH short line ($2,050), the BTC neutral gate ($76K) — and keep dry powder for the Monday Asian cash open when liquidity returns and the week's catalysts (SUI unlock, ISM, then the NFP labour week) start firing.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear), Δ +1 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm
HackerNews trending crypto-tagged: "A Gentle Introduction to Lattice-Based Cryptography [pdf]" (↑169)

WEEKEND THIN-BOOK TELL — it's Sunday, the last day of May; ForexFactory shows no high-impact USD events, so any sharp wick today is liquidity-driven, not catalyst-driven. Single large players move the weekend tape; do not mistake a thin-book sweep for a real trend. The screen shows price but not the missing depth behind it — fade nothing, confirm everything on Monday.

SUI UNLOCK FRONT-RUN — SUI is already -14.8% over 7d INTO tomorrow's ~44M-token (~1.3% supply) unlock. The screen shows weakness; what it isn't showing is that the weakness IS the positioning — crowded shorts pre-front-running the cliff set up a violent sell-the-rumor / buy-the-news squeeze if $0.88 holds and reclaims on the first Monday cash candle.

HBAR STRUCTURAL INFLOW vs THIN FLOAT — the lone green name (+8.6% 7d) is bid on Canary spot-ETF AUM (~$93M) + freshly-listed CME HBAR futures, not on retail momentum. Against a small circulating float, persistent ETF creation amplifies upside; a $0.105 reclaim with volume is the trigger the price screen alone won't telegraph until it's already through.

ETF FLOW DIVERGENCE — May closed with ~$2.07B of net BTC spot-ETF OUTFLOWS (heaviest monthly since launch) AND ETH on a 12-13 session outflow streak. The price screen shows a quiet range; the flow screen shows institutions stepping back. Range-bound price on heavy redemptions is distribution, not accumulation — the tell precedes the breakdown.

ETH STAKING ATH UNDER A SAGGING PRICE — staked ETH just hit ~39.5M (all-time high) even as price pins under $2,000 on outflows. Supply is leaving liquid float while price falls — a coiled spring that only releases on a flow reversal. Until the ETF outflow streak breaks, the staking ATH is latent energy, not a buy signal.

F&G STABILISING, NOT CAPITULATING — Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) is UP from 23 the prior session. The screen's red candles suggest panic; the sentiment gauge says the panic already peaked and is grinding back. A rising F&G off a low into a known supply event (SUI unlock) is the setup for a relief bounce IF Monday liquidity confirms — not a reason to short into the hole.

NFP LABOUR WEEK IS THE REAL MACRO PIVOT — the screen is fixated on the SUI unlock, but the bigger driver lands Fri Jun 5: Non-Farm Payrolls, the first major test of the Warsh-Fed rate path. ADP (Wed) + Jobless Claims (Thu) pre-load the read. A soft labour print revives the rate-cut narrative the ETF-outflow tape is currently denying — that's the basket-wide bid nobody's pricing yet.

CLARITY IS PRICED OUT, NOT PRICED IN — XRP's 15-9 Senate Banking win (May 14) is fully discounted; the full-floor vote is an August story. The screen still carries the regulatory-tailwind headline, but it's inert for weeks. Watch instead whether XRP OUTPERFORMS BTC on any basket bounce — that relative-strength tell is the only live signal that the $1.41B ETF-flow bid is still working underneath.
